mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 35i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N55 3.0-litre engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £3,500–£5,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 35i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N55 is a proven, powerful engine — but it has known weak points: HPFP failure, chargepipe cracking, oil leaks from the valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets, and water pump failure. The engine itself is robust when properly maintained, but these issues are common and should be budgeted for.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 35i?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are HPFP failure (long cranking, hesitation, stalling), chargepipe failure (plastic cracks, boost leaks, loss of power), oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket, water pump failure, and timing chain tensioner wear.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 35i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £18,000 X3 35i, a £6,000 recon is 33% — worth it. Against a tired £8,000 example, the same recon approaches our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 35i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The X3 35i carries the engine code N55B30A. It's a 3.0-litre straight-six turbo petrol engine used in the F25 X3 from 2011 to 2017.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the HPFP, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — HPFP replacement is a well-understood repair costing £600–£1,200. It's the N55's most common weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — but it's a straightforward fix, not an engine killer.

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure
problems.symptoms: Long cranking on startup, hesitation under acceleration, engine stalling, loss of power, engine warning light, rough idling, misfires.
problems.recommendation: The N55's HPFP is its most notorious weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — a failing HPFP can lead to lean running and catalytic converter damage. The replacement is well-understood and well-documented. If the HPFP fails completely, symptoms include rough idling, misfires, and a Check Engine Light.

failureLink.label: Read the full HPFP failure guide

problems.title: Chargepipe Failure
problems.symptoms: Loss of boost, whistling noise from the engine bay, engine warning light, reduced power, poor running conditions.
problems.recommendation: The N55's plastic chargepipe is prone to cracking with age and constant heat cycles. The factory plastic pipes become brittle and often crack, resulting in boost leaks, check engine lights, and poor running conditions. The solution is an aftermarket metal chargepipe — fit once and forget.

failureLink.label: Read the full chargepipe failure guide

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Valve Cover Gasket & Oil Filter Housing Gasket)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the car, burning oil smell from the engine bay, low oil level warning, visible oil residue around the valve cover or oil filter housing area.
problems.recommendation: Oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket are common on the N55. The plastic valve cover becomes brittle from engine heat and typically needs replacement around 100,000 miles. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the engine inspected promptly. Left unchecked, oil loss can lead to low oil levels and potential engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 35i? Check These First
buyIf: * Chargepipe replaced with aftermarket metal unit
* Valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket replaced within the last 40,000 miles
* Full service history with BMW LL-01 approved oil
* No cold-start rattle, misfires, or hesitation
avoidIf: * No proof of HPFP maintenance — this is a when, not an if
* Oil leaks with no documented gasket replacement history
* Poor service history — the N55 is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X3 35i is powered by BMW's N55 — a proven, powerful straight-six turbo that represents a significant step forward from the twin-turbo N54. It's a genuinely enjoyable engine in the X3, offering 306 PS and 400 Nm of torque. But it's not bulletproof — HPFP failure, chargepipe cracking, oil leaks, and water pump issues are the price of entry. These are well-understood, manageable fixes. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained 35i is a £15,000–£25,000 premium SUV; a neglected one is a £7,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare 35i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 35i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for long cranking or hesitation (HPFP warning)
* Service History — including the N55 era (2011–2017) and confirmed oil-change intervals using BMW LL-01 approved oil
* HPFP Check — Any history of long cranking, hesitation, or HPFP replacement?
* Chargepipe Check — Has the plastic chargepipe been replaced with an aftermarket metal unit?
* Oil Leak Check — Any evidence of oil leaks around the valve cover or oil filter housing?
* Coolant Check — Any history of coolant loss or water pump replacement?
* Smoke Check — White or blue smoke on startup can indicate PCV or turbo issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved HPFP, chargepipe, or cooling system issues?
